zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby於1218年出生在英國哈普爾維古姆時，她的父親Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester23歲，母親Helen de Galloway18歲。她於1238年與Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1284年3月12日在英國米德萊可可拉肯維爾享年66歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾梅列瓦列。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby于1218年出生在英国哈普爾維古姆时，她的父亲Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester23岁，母亲Helen de Galloway18岁。她于1238年与Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby在英国英格兰林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1284年3月12日在英国米德萊可可拉肯維爾享年66岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾梅列瓦列。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Quincy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in France deriving their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Quintus, meaning ‘fifth(-born)’ + the locative suffix -acum. The earliest bearer of the name in England, that of Saer de Quincy, ancestor of the earls of Winchester, was from Cuinchy in Pas-de-Calais, but the name may have been reinforced by immigrants from Quincy-sous-Sénard in Seine-et-Oise or Quincy-Voisins in Seine-et-Marne. A family of the name de Quincy held the title Earl of Winchester 1207–65. Descendants of this family later bore the name Quincey up to the time of the English essayist Thomas De Quincey (1785–1859), who changed his name from Quincey to De Quincey in 1796.

History: The American Quincy family were established in MA by Edmund Quincy in 1633. Fifth in descent was Josiah Quincy (1744–75), a leading patriot, who was sent to England to argue the colonists’ case in 1774. His son Josiah (1772–1864) was a powerful opponent of slavery, president of Harvard, and mayor of Boston, a post also held by several of his descendants. The traditional pronunciation is “Quinzy”.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby was born in 1218, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, her father, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, was 23 and her mother, Helen de Galloway, was 18. She married Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby in 1238, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 12 March 1284, in Clerkenwell, Middlesex, England, at the age of 66, and was buried in Merevale, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby est née en 1218, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Helen de Galloway, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby en 1238, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 mars 1284, à Clerkenwell, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Merevale, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derbyが1218年にイギリスハンプシュアイアウィンチェスターで生まれた時，父Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchesterは23歳，母Helen de Gallowayは18歳であった。1238年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでSir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derbyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1284年3月12日にイギリスミッドレセックスクラーケンウエルで66歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアメレベルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby(이)가 1218년 영국 함프실르 윈체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helen de Galloway(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1238년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에서 Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1284년 3월 12일 영국 미들레세스 클레르켄웰에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 머에발에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Quincy Countess of Derby родилась в 1218 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Helen de Galloway, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby в 1238 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 12 марта 1284 г. в местности клиркенвелл, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности миревеил, варвиккшир, Англия.

Se casó con Sir William de Ferrers 5th Earl of Derby en 1238, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 12 de marzo de 1284, en Clerkenwell, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en Merevale, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
